notes: Shell-escape command for Gemini CLI, asserting the CORRECT (target) arc — KNOWN_FAILING today (daemon=bug). The recipe keys-sends a `!echo hello` shell-escape in the REPL; Gemini runs it in a LOCAL shell with NO LLM round-trip (handleShellCommand → ShellExecutionService.execute, never chat.sendMessage). DAEMON STORY (grounded in a live recording, supersedes a doc-only guess): Gemini PERSISTS the shell-escape to its session rollout (~/.gemini/tmp/<project>/chats/session-*.jsonl) as an ordinary `type:"user"` text message ('I ran the following shell command: ... This produced the following result: ...'), with NO following `gemini` assistant message. Today parser.go::parseUser classifies that as `user_message` → ready→working, and since no terminal `gemini` text turn ever lands the parser.go:209 turn_done settle never fires — so the session STICKS in working. That violates the scenario invariant ('does not stick in working after the shell command exits'). CORRECT behavior (what these phases assert and what the fix must deliver): recognize the shell-escape preamble and skip it (mirroring claudecode's isLocalCommandContent skip of <bash-input>/<bash-stdout>, parser.go:216-225), so state STAYS ready throughout the shell-escape window. shell_escape_window asserts that. A final normal turn proves the session is alive and a real prompt still drives the ready→working→ready arc. SESSION IDENTITY: Gemini stores its transcript as a *.jsonl rollout under ~/.gemini/tmp/<project>/chats/ (FilesUnderRoot, keyed by sessionId/UUID), launcher PID binds via cwd from the bootstrap <session_context> (heap-bump V8 re-exec excluded by isHeapBumpWorker). session_birth anchors to recording start UNPINNED so a transient proc-<PID> presession can't steal birth. DRIVER GAP: the shell-escape is sent via a `keys` step (raw tmux send-keys, does NOT bump EXPECTED_TURNS) — claudecode's reference pattern — because send_text would desync the trailing wait_turn turn count; the gemini-cli driver's keys arm is stubbed (driver=gap:keys) and record ports it before driving. Observations assert the websocket metric vector: cost and tokens non-zero (the final real turn bills input/output) and agent gemini-cli; model is left unpinned because the live default is account-dependent.

text: Session appears in ready shortly after gemini -y launches
Launcher PID bound via cwd from the bootstrap <session_context> (heap-bump worker excluded)

After the !echo shell-escape runs locally (no LLM round-trip), state must STAY ready — the parser should skip the shell-escape user-preamble (mirroring claudecode's <bash-input>/<bash-stdout> skip). KNOWN-FAILING today: the parser instead drives ready→working with no settle, sticking in working. This phase asserts the corrected behavior the bug fix must deliver.
After the shell-escape window, a normal user prompt drives ready→working — proves the session is still alive and a real prompt still opens a turn
The terminal 'alive' gemini text turn (non-empty content, zero toolCalls → turn_done) settles working→ready; state never entered waiting
REPL torn down once after the final turn → a single process_exited on the session; state immediately before teardown is ready
